tasks.title: Add refutations_screened to seed-schema.json verification object
tasks.description: Add an optional integer field `refutations_screened` to the verification object definition in `~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/contracts/seed-schema.json`. The verification object currently has `additionalProperties: false`, so the field must be added explicitly to the properties block. The change is additive and non-breaking: existing seeds without the field must continue to validate. After editing, run validate-schema.sh against at least one existing seed file to confirm backward compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: seed-schema.json verification object includes refutations_screened as an optional integer property
Running validate-schema.sh against docs/seeds/20260604-2052-judge-escalation-ladder.json (which lacks the field) returns exit 0
A synthetic seed JSON with verification.refutations_screened set to an integer passes validate-schema.sh
A synthetic seed with verification.refutations_screened set to a string fails validate-schema.sh

tasks.title: Implement escalation ladder in to-seed SKILL.md step 3c
tasks.description: Rewrite step 3c in `~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/to-seed/SKILL.md` to implement the two-round judge escalation ladder. For each refutation returned by the persona agents: (1) spawn one Sonnet judge (round 1); (2) if round-1 returns `upheld`, spawn 3 fresh Sonnet judges in parallel and apply 2-of-3 majority to determine the final verdict — the round-1 verdict is NOT reused as a vote; (3) if round-1 returns `rejected`, increment the local refutations_screened counter and move to the next refutation. After processing all refutations, write the final refutations_screened count into the verification stamp alongside the existing fields. The short-circuit rule (all personas return empty refutations → skip judge stage entirely) must remain in place and take priority over the escalation ladder.
acceptance_criteria: Step 3c text describes the round-1 single-judge spawn with explicit Sonnet model
Step 3c text describes the lone-upheld → 3 fresh Sonnet judges parallel path with 2-of-3 majority
Step 3c text describes the lone-rejected → refutations_screened increment → next refutation path
Step 3c text states the round-1 verdict is not reused in the escalated panel
Step 3c text states the short-circuit (all personas return []) still skips judge stage entirely
The verification stamp section includes refutations_screened as an output field

tasks.title: Smoke test escalation ladder on a live to-seed run (HITL)
tasks.description: Run /to-seed on a real grill session or handoff file that is expected to produce at least one persona refutation. Observe the verification stage to confirm: round-1 fires one judge per refutation; a lone upheld triggers the 3-judge parallel panel; a lone rejected increments refutations_screened without spawning additional judges; the output seed's verification stamp contains the refutations_screened field with the correct count. Also run /to-seed on a session where all personas return empty refutations to confirm the short-circuit still skips the judge stage entirely.
acceptance_criteria: A live to-seed run with at least one refutation completes without errors
Workflow logs show a single round-1 judge agent firing per refutation
At least one lone-upheld escalation triggers 3 parallel judge agents (visible in workflow progress)
Output seed verification stamp contains refutations_screened integer matching the number of lone-reject terminations
A to-seed run where all personas return [] completes and the verification stamp shows 0 judge agents fired

follow_ups.title: Calibrate escalation threshold using refutations_screened data
steps: After 5+ real to-seed runs, collect refutations_screened values from output seeds: `grep -r 'refutations_screened' ~/.dotfiles/docs/seeds/ | grep -o '[0-9]\+$'`
Calculate the ratio: refutations_screened / total refutations across those runs. If most refutations escalate (ratio < 0.3), the round-1 screener is saving minimal compute.
If ratio < 0.3: consider opening a new grill to evaluate whether the evidence-bound constraint is too permissive (lone rejects rarely fire → round-1 judge rarely terminates early).
If ratio > 0.7: the screener is working well; no changes needed.
Record findings as a further_notes entry in the next to-seed session that touches the verification pipeline.
